methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Bob Mumgaard, has raised $2.863B to date and carries a stated post-money valuation of $5.0B+. The peer universe consists of five private Climate & Energy companies at Series B stage, with a median valuation of $310M and a range of $120M to $500M. The comparison set includes Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M), alongside one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x applied to the $5.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $9.5B. The peer-median method examines the five same-stage, same-sector comparables, producing a median implied valuation of $310M. The substantial divergence reflects Commonwealth's outlier capital raise and stated valuation relative to its peer cohort.

Triangulation across both methods produces a wide range: low of $264M, median of $4.9B, and high of $10.9B. The marked disparity between stage-progression results ($9.5B) and peer-median results ($310M) reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Commonwealth's $5B+ current valuation is justified by peer trading levels or supported by step-up dynamics. Further clarity on capital deployment efficiency and technology de-risking would be required to narrow this range.
